title: fix: h100-mfu-calculation
body: I was getting 104% MFU on h100 then i realized that MFU calculation might have been based on a100 312 tflops h100 is 989 tflops at bf16. Nvidia claims 1,979 tflops but that is for 2:4 sparsity.

step_plan.summary: Fix MFU calculation by updating the MFU denominator from the A100 bf16 baseline to the H100 bf16 peak baseline and document the rationale to ensure MFU readings reflect actual hardware capabilities.

steps.operation: Update MFU denominator to reflect H100 bf16 peak
steps.reason: To align MFU calculation with the actual hardware baseline (H100 bf16 peak ~989e12 FLOPS) instead of the A100 bf16 baseline (312e12 FLOPS), preventing MFU readings from exceeding 100% on H100 systems.
steps.side_effects: MFU outputs during training will shift to reflect the new baseline; any tests or dashboards asserting exact MFU values tied to the old 312e12 baseline may need updates; ensures comments/docs explain the rationale to prevent regression; groundwork left for a future knob to switch baselines if sparsity-based numbers are desired.
